target: WIN-058 detail_evidence and finding in data/wins.json — defense neutralization (Mode 3, curmudgeon c3 Advocate defense_survives=3)

patches.replacement: θ = −lonE is a relabeling of the centuries-old east-longitude convention — any polar-azimuthal projection of the globe satisfies it automatically. No dome prediction requires it: every distance, bearing, and timing the dome computes from θ is reproduced by standard spherical coordinates using the same WGS-84 longitude input. V13 promotes geographic longitude directly to θ, cleaning up V9's solar-noon-UTC proxy — but V12's 0.9941 EW scale factor persists live in distance_analysis.py, so two incompatible angular conventions now coexist without reconciliation. WGS-84 dependency persists (OPEN-001), and accuracy (7.3%) is 600–700× worse than Vincenty geodesy. Even read charitably as a model-consistency test, the identity distinguishes only the polar-azimuthal projection class — a family the dome was already committed to by its disc-centered geometry — so it cannot discriminate between the dome and any globe-based polar-centered projection using the same WGS-84 inputs.

patches.change: insert 'Preempting the consistency-test defense' block before the 'Registry-integrity-notes — documentary acknowledgement (2026-04-17)' block
patches.inserted_block: Preempting the consistency-test defense. A charitable dome defender can reframe WIN-058 as not a claim of novel identity but a model-consistency test — “does the V13 coordinate system recover the correct angles in both hemispheres with a single parameterless equation?” — and argue that θ = −lonE discriminates the dome's polar-azimuthal projection from alternative projection classes that would fail the same test (Mercator-off-pole, equatorial cylindrical, any non-polar-centered map). Credit where due: that is the strongest defense available. The identity does hold automatically on polar-azimuthal projections and breaks on non-polar ones, so narrowly the defense names a real discriminating property, and the V13 fix does represent legitimate engineering progress over the V9 solar-noon-UTC proxy. What the defense misses is that the dome was already committed to the polar-azimuthal projection class before V13 — the North-Pole-centered disc geometry is an architectural axiom of the model, not an outcome of any coordinate test. The 'test' therefore compares the dome to projections the dome's own geometry had already excluded, and passing a consistency check against an axiom the model already committed to is unpacking, not prediction. Worse, any globe-based polar azimuthal equidistant projection — standard cartography since Snyder (1987) Map Projections: A Working Manual, ch. 25, and known in closed form since the 19th century — satisfies θ = −lonE identically using the same WGS-84 longitude inputs the dome relies on. So the identity discriminates only polar from non-polar projections, a distinction orthogonal to the flat-vs-curved question the dome needs it to settle. Operationally: swap the prime-meridian convention (for example, adopt the Paris meridian in force before 1884) and every θ shifts by exactly the offset, so the relationship θ = −lonE is really θ(WGS-84) = −lonE(WGS-84) — a tautology about the coordinate input the dome takes from standard geodesy, not a property of the dome. The defender's 'weak constraint, but weak is not zero' framing therefore concedes the case: the constraint is tight only against projection classes the dome was never in, and zero against every polar-centered geometry — flat or spherical — that reads longitude from the same datum. That is not a Weak Prediction; it is a definitional identity inside the projection family the dome had already chosen.
patches.replacement:
WIN-058 claims that the angular coordinate in the dome frame is exactly θ = −lonE (negation of geographic east longitude), unifying Northern and Southern Hemisphere coordinate derivation. The dome presents this as a discovery: the V9-era formula used solar noon UTC as a proxy for θ (V12 partially corrected this with a 0.9941 EW angular scale factor, but errors persisted in the Southern Hemisphere), producing catastrophic errors in the Southern Hemisphere (Sydney: +32.5° instead of the correct −151.2°, an ≈176° error (minimum angular distance on the circle between +32.5° and −151.2°)). The V13 fix recognizes that solar noon UTC ≈ 12 − lonE/15h, so θ = (noonUTC − 12) × 15° = −lonE. The dome calls this a 'confirmed prediction.'

Kernel of truth: The dome correctly identifies a real bug in its own V9-era coordinate system (acknowledged and partially corrected in V12 with the 0.9941 scale factor, then fully resolved in V13). The solar-noon-UTC proxy was mathematically equivalent to −lonE for Northern Hemisphere cities (where noon falls near 12:00 UTC) but broke catastrophically for Southern Hemisphere cities whose solar noon times produce angles in the wrong quadrant. Recognizing and fixing this is genuine model improvement — the V13 formula eliminates 180°+ errors in Southern Hemisphere cities.

Why this is not a prediction: The identity θ = −lonE is not derived from dome geometry. It is derived from the definition of longitude: geographic east longitude measures the angle east of Greenwich, and the dome's angular coordinate measures the angle west of Greenwich (since the dome's θ increases clockwise in the AE projection). The relationship θ = −lonE is a coordinate convention that follows from how longitude is defined — it is true on any map projection centered on the North Pole, not specifically on a flat disc. No dome-specific physics (disc radius, firmament height, aetheric wave speed) enters the derivation. The dome did not predict this relationship; it adopted the existing geographic convention it was already implicitly using.

The bug it fixes was self-inflicted: The 183° Sydney error existed only because V12 used solar noon UTC as an indirect proxy instead of longitude directly. Geographic east longitude has been available since the 18th century (following the adoption of the Greenwich meridian). The dome's V12 system introduced an unnecessary indirection (solar noon → θ) that garbled Southern Hemisphere angles. V13's 'discovery' that θ = −lonE is equivalent to recognizing that the proxy was unnecessary — the direct quantity was available all along. Correcting a self-inflicted bug is maintenance, not prediction.

WGS84 dependency persists: The θ = −lonE formula takes geographic east longitude as input. Geographic east longitude is defined by the WGS84 geodetic datum (or its predecessors). The dome's own OPEN-001 concedes: 'Dome-native coordinates without WGS84 — V12 uses longitude × 0.9941 as proxy.' V13 replaces the 0.9941 scaling with a direct longitude negation, but the WGS84 dependency remains — the dome still needs globe-derived coordinates as input. The dome's AI context page (V50.10) still lists 'EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation' under OPEN-003, and the distance computation code (distance_analysis.py) still applies the 0.9941 factor: delta_t = np.radians(t2 - t1) * 0.9941. The V13 angular identity and the V12 scaling factor coexist in the codebase without reconciliation.

Performance context: V13's θ = −lonE achieves 7.3% mean error for Northern Hemisphere same-hemisphere routes — a regression from V12's 5.2%. Cross-equatorial RMSE improved from 25–78% to 6.2%, but this improvement comes from the two-zone topology (WIN-057's rSH = 2 × 14,105 − rNH), not from the angular coordinate fix alone. Standard geodesy (Vincenty inverse formula on the WGS84 ellipsoid) achieves sub-0.01% accuracy globally with two parameters derived from rotational physics. The dome's coordinate system, even after the V13 fix, is 600–700× less accurate than the system it takes its inputs from (best-case: 6.2% cross-equatorial RMSE vs sub-0.01% gives ~620×; worst-case: 7.3% NH mean error gives ~730×). Near-equatorial cities remain excluded entirely: the dome's own OPEN-015 concedes Singapore (r = 23,556 km) 'falls beyond the equatorial ring' at req = 14,105 km.

Preempting the consistency-test defense. A charitable dome defender can reframe WIN-058 as not a claim of novel identity but a model-consistency test — “does the V13 coordinate system recover the correct angles in both hemispheres with a single parameterless equation?” — and argue that θ = −lonE discriminates the dome's polar-azimuthal projection from alternative projection classes that would fail the same test (Mercator-off-pole, equatorial cylindrical, any non-polar-centered map). Credit where due: that is the strongest defense available. The identity does hold automatically on polar-azimuthal projections and breaks on non-polar ones, so narrowly the defense names a real discriminating property, and the V13 fix does represent legitimate engineering progress over the V9 solar-noon-UTC proxy. What the defense misses is that the dome was already committed to the polar-azimuthal projection class before V13 — the North-Pole-centered disc geometry is an architectural axiom of the model, not an outcome of any coordinate test. The 'test' therefore compares the dome to projections the dome's own geometry had already excluded, and passing a consistency check against an axiom the model already committed to is unpacking, not prediction. Worse, any globe-based polar azimuthal equidistant projection — standard cartography since Snyder (1987) Map Projections: A Working Manual, ch. 25, and known in closed form since the 19th century — satisfies θ = −lonE identically using the same WGS-84 longitude inputs the dome relies on. So the identity discriminates only polar from non-polar projections, a distinction orthogonal to the flat-vs-curved question the dome needs it to settle. Operationally: swap the prime-meridian convention (for example, adopt the Paris meridian in force before 1884) and every θ shifts by exactly the offset, so the relationship θ = −lonE is really θ(WGS-84) = −lonE(WGS-84) — a tautology about the coordinate input the dome takes from standard geodesy, not a property of the dome. The defender's 'weak constraint, but weak is not zero' framing therefore concedes the case: the constraint is tight only against projection classes the dome was never in, and zero against every polar-centered geometry — flat or spherical — that reads longitude from the same datum. That is not a Weak Prediction; it is a definitional identity inside the projection family the dome had already chosen.

Registry-integrity-notes — documentary acknowledgement (2026-04-17): On 2026-04-17 the dome author added a registry_integrity_notes block to docs/api/current/claim_index.json (V51.1, last_updated 2026-04-17), acknowledging that wins.html renders two different claims under the visible label 'WIN-058'. Verbatim: 'Two different claims share this visible label in wins.html. The API disambiguates them as win-058-bedrock-kappa and win-058-theta-rule.' This θ = −lonE rule is now keyed win-058-theta-rule (status: confirmed; domain: geometry_coordinates); the bedrock-kappa claim our review tracks as WIN-058b is keyed win-058-bedrock-kappa (the entry's status field was set to pending_independent_verification when the key was added, with an explicit honest_caveat that 'independent raw-data reproduction or a successful August 12 2026 Tier 3 result is still required').

Credit where due: the dome author identified the label collision our review had been flagging and shipped a machine-readable fix inside 24 hours of the reactive-change wave. The ai_manifest.json recommended_read_order directs AI auditors to ai_manifest → methodology → review-response → claim_index → wins.html, so an agent that follows that order will encounter the disambiguation before reaching the duplicated visible label. For that audience the collision is resolved.

The visible prose is unchanged. On the live wins.html fetched 2026-04-18, two separate blocks still render with the WIN-058 badge — one for the 1.67 bedrock-kappa claim ('The 1.67 Bedrock Induction Ratio', badge PENDING INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION) and one for this θ = −lonE rule ('Unified Angular Coordinate Rule', badge CONFIRMED 2026-03-28). Human readers do not follow the ai_manifest.json read-order, and the site's headline ('69 confirmed') is tallied against the visible-prose registry, not the disambiguated API. The acknowledgement therefore stops at the machine layer; the user-facing surface still carries the duplicate label for two different claims — one confirmed, one pending. The disambiguation is machine-only.

Pattern — acknowledge-without-fix, at least three instances in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17). This is one of at least three parallel documentary concession the dome author shipped on 2026-04-17: (i) methodology.json timestamp_scope — the OpenTimestamps scope concession covered in the Timestamp Error tab April 2026 update block; (ii) methodology.json registry_counts — the '94.5%' ratio disavowed at the API layer ('bookkeeping, not a substitute for claim-by-claim audit') while still headlined on index.html (open issue ISS-1087); (iii) this registry_integrity_notes block. Three acknowledgements in a single commit, zero structural fixes to the user-facing site. Each fix lives exactly where an AI auditor is told to look and nowhere a human reader will see. The pattern is stronger evidence of intent than any single acknowledgement in isolation. A fair version of our critique therefore credits the documentary progress while noting that the presentation layer — and the headline number that depends on it — has not been touched.

holes_addressed: curmudgeon c3 advocate_mode.defense_survives=3: 'WIN-058 is a model-consistency test, not a novel-identity claim; θ = −lonE discriminates polar-azimuthal projections from non-polar ones, so a fair verdict is Weak Prediction not Misleading.' Now preempted in both finding (one-sentence closer) and detail_evidence (full dedicated block).
new_evidence_added: Explicit citation of Snyder (1987) Map Projections: A Working Manual, USGS Professional Paper 1395, chapter 25 (Azimuthal Equidistant Projection, polar aspect) as the canonical 19th-century derivation of θ = −lonE on polar AE projections.

Structural-argument framing: the dome's polar-azimuthal projection class is an architectural axiom (disc centered on North Pole), committed before any V13 coordinate test — so the 'consistency test' is axiom unpacking, not prediction.

Operational circularity demonstration: swapping the reference meridian (e.g., Paris pre-1884) shifts every θ by exactly the offset, confirming θ = −lonE is a property of the WGS-84 input convention, not of the dome.
Explicit naming of the projection class the identity fails to distinguish: any globe-based polar AE projection with the same WGS-84 longitude input reproduces θ = −lonE identically.

anticipated_objections.objection: But weak constraints still count — ruling out non-polar projections is doing real work, even if the constraint is narrow.
anticipated_objections.response: Only if the dome was ever a candidate for a non-polar projection. It wasn't — polar-centered is an architectural axiom of the disc geometry, not an outcome the model could have gotten wrong. A test that rules out projection classes the dome was never in has zero discriminating power over the choice the dome actually made. The test also fails to distinguish the dome from a globe-based polar AE projection, which is the discrimination the 'flat vs globe' debate actually needs.

anticipated_objections.objection: V13 genuinely fixed the V9 solar-noon-UTC bug — that's real model improvement and deserves partial credit.
anticipated_objections.response: Granted, and we credit that progress in the Kernel of Truth and in the V9→V12→V13 trajectory paragraph. But fixing a self-inflicted bug by adopting a centuries-old cartographic identity is maintenance, not prediction. The V13 claim is listed on wins.html as CONFIRMED (2026-03-28) — a label that asserts a successful prediction. A 'Weak Prediction' label would need to be earned by pre-registration of θ = −lonE before the dome chose polar-centered geometry. The actual chronology is the opposite.

anticipated_objections.objection: A globe-based polar AE projection also uses WGS-84 longitude, so θ = −lonE is no more circular for the dome than for standard geodesy.
anticipated_objections.response: Exactly the point. The identity is not distinguishing the dome from the globe. It is a shared property of both. That is why the 'confirmation' cannot discriminate the models; it confirms only that both use the same convention for measuring angle west of Greenwich. Globe geodesy is up-front about this (it is a convention); the dome frames it as a confirmed prediction, which is where the 'Misleading' verdict comes from.

anticipated_objections.objection: Even a non-discriminating identity can be part of a larger chain of evidence — you can't dismiss it in isolation.
anticipated_objections.response: Agreed that no single WIN is decisive. But this WIN is specifically labelled 'CONFIRMED' with a date badge as if it had been tested. The Misleading verdict refers to the presentation (confirmation badge on a convention, not a test), not to the underlying identity. If the dome relabeled it 'Convention Adopted' or 'Coordinate Consistency Check' we would not call it Misleading.

kernel_of_truth: The V9 → V12 → V13 trajectory is real engineering progress — each version removed a real bug (V9's ~180° Sydney error in the Southern Hemisphere, V12's lingering 0.9941 EW scale factor and SH proxy). The Advocate is also correct on a narrow technical point: θ = −lonE holds automatically on polar-centered azimuthal projections and breaks on non-polar ones, so the identity does name a real discriminating property against projection classes like Mercator-off-pole or equatorial cylindrical. Where the defense fails is that the dome was committed to the polar-azimuthal class before V13 by architectural axiom (disc centered on the North Pole), so the 'consistency test' compares the dome only to projections its own geometry had already excluded; and any globe-based polar AE projection using the same WGS-84 longitude input satisfies θ = −lonE identically (Snyder 1987 ch. 25), so the identity cannot discriminate flat from spherical geometry — only polar from non-polar.
analyst_note_on_rating: Curmudgeon rated defense 3/5 ('requires preemptive text'). Analyst rates it 2/5: the defense concedes the identity is non-novel and asks only for a verdict relabel (Misleading → Weak Prediction). The structural argument that polar-centered geometry is an architectural axiom (not a test outcome) collapses the defense — passing a consistency check against an axiom the model already committed to is axiom unpacking, not prediction, weak or otherwise. The verdict stays 'Misleading' because the 'CONFIRMED' badge on the live site asserts a successful predictive test that the identity cannot be. However, the defense is worth preempting in-text (rating 2/5, not 1/5) because the narrow discriminating-property point is technically correct and a defender can plausibly construct it from a quick read; leaving it unaddressed invites the Weak-Prediction rebrand.

defense_neutralization.notes: Defense's narrow discriminating-property point (θ = −lonE distinguishes polar-azimuthal from non-polar projections) is conceded as a kernel of truth; neutralization shows the dome was already committed to the polar-azimuthal class by architectural axiom and that any globe-based polar AE projection satisfies the same identity, so the test cannot discriminate flat from spherical geometry. Preemption lives in finding (one-sentence closer) and detail_evidence (dedicated block).

cross_references: WIN-057 (two-zone topology r_SH = 2×14,105 − r_NH — a second layered coordinate treatment on top of θ = −lonE)
tldr_update_rationale: Current tldr_evidence ('...it's not a discovery, it's a coordinate convention. The dome was using a broken proxy (solar-noon-UTC) and fixed it.') and tldr_verdict ('θ = −lonE follows from the definition of longitude, not from dome physics. Fixing a self-inflicted coordinate bug is model maintenance, not a confirmed prediction.') already capture the core argument at the layperson level. The Advocate consistency-test defense is a specialist-level rhetorical move — it belongs in the detail layer, not the TLDR. A fair non-specialist reader does not reach for projection-class discrimination.
